title: The China Show 6/26/2026 (Video)
Oil Heads for Weekly Loss as Ship Attack Clouds Hormuz Outlook
summary: Oil was on track for a weekly decline after transits through the Strait of Hormuz accelerated, although an attack on a cargo ship has renewed concerns about safe passage through the vital waterway.

title: Apple Price Hikes Spark Asia Tech Selloff on Memory Cost Concern
summary: Asian technology stocks slumped after Apple raised prices for its products, stoking concern that rising component prices will curb demand for devices and eventually slow the memory chip rally that has powered much of the AI trade.

title: Renewed Tech Selloff Jolts Asian Stocks, Oil Slips: Markets Wrap
summary: Asian stocks slumped to a two-week low as the tech sector came under renewed pressure after a selloff in Apple Inc.’s shares and a report that OpenAI may delay its initial public offering soured sentiment.

title: GSMA Ltd. CEO Hoffman on Mobile Technology
summary: John Hoffman, the CEO of GSMA, says China is well positioned to shape the next era of communications, particularly in the field of agentic AI. He speaks on the sidelines of Mobile World Congress in Shanghai. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Lingyi iTech Rises in Hong Kong Debut
summary: Kathy Zeng, Chairwoman of Apple supplier Lingyi iTech, says the company has secured major clients in both the US and China as it expands into the humanoid robotics sector. She speaks with Bloomberg's Minmin Low on "Bloomberg: The China Show". (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Ship Struck in Hormuz as Oil Supertankers Turn Back Again
summary: A ship was hit by an unidentified projectile in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday in the first reported attack since an interim US-Iran peace deal, marking a setback to efforts to restore traffic through the vital energy thoroughfare.

title: Gold Heads for Weekly Loss as Tech Rout Adds to Rate Concerns
summary: Gold dipped back below $4,000 an ounce and was on track for a fourth weekly loss as a tech-led selloff in Asian stocks added to concerns around prospects for interest-rate hikes.

title: Canada’s Quebecor Makes Last Push to Thwart Corus Debt Swap
summary: Quebecor Inc. urged Canada’s broadcasting regulator to hold a public hearing on the proposed takeover of Corus Entertainment Inc. by its lenders — arguing that a better option is to allow Quebecor to buy the heavily indebted media company.

title: Drone Startup Elroy Air Is Said to Near $800 Million SPAC Deal
summary: Elroy Air Inc., a cargo drone startup aiming to replace delivery trucks, is in advanced talks to go public through a merger with a blank-check vehicle that will create a company with an enterprise value of about $1 billion.

title: Korean Stocks Tumble 9% on Chip Selloff, Triggers Trading Halt
summary: South Korean stocks were hit by a second trading suspension this week as chipmakers plunged, highlighting the market’s sensitivity to swings in global artificial-intelligence sentiment.

title: Tech Volatility Spills Into Asia | The Asia Trade 6/26/2026
summary: "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Empty LNG Tankers Mass Outside Qatar as Exports Tick Higher
summary: Empty liquefied natural gas tankers are lining up outside of Qatar’s massive export plant in the Persian Gulf as the supplier seeks to quickly increase production following early progress in US-Iran peace talks.

title: China’s Collapsing Crude Oil Imports to Fall Further in June
summary: The world’s top crude oil importer is set to take even fewer barrels this month, extending its exceptionally subdued appetite since the start of the Iran war.

title: Aluminum Heads for Fourth Weekly Drop on Mideast Supply Return
summary: Aluminum headed for a fourth weekly decline, the longest losing streak since April 2025, as a renewed selloff in Asian tech stocks added to pressure from a stronger US dollar and returning Middle East supplies.

title: Philippines, UAE and Indonesia to Start CPTPP Accession Talks
summary: The Philippines, United Arab Emirates and Indonesia will start talks about joining a regional trade bloc, the next step for the three in efforts to expand free-trade ties.

title: Apple Supplier Lingyi Rises in HK After $1.1 Billion Debut
summary: Lingyi iTech Guangdong Co. a Chinese electronic supplier for Apple Inc. and Tesla Inc., rose in its Hong Kong debut after raising HK$8.3 billion ($1.06 billion) in a share sale, part of the city’s busiest month for listings this year.

title: Hong Kong Dollar Hits 10-Month Low as Fed View Buoys Greenback
summary: The Hong Kong dollar fell to its weakest level against the US dollar in about 10 months, as a stronger greenback and expectations for further Federal Reserve rate hikes pressured Asian currencies.

title: Iron Ore Set for Seventh Straight Weekly Loss as Market Softens
summary: Iron ore headed for a seventh weekly loss — the worst run since 2022 — as demand weakened seasonally and mill margins narrowed.

title: Sherritt Warns of Going-Concern Risk After Trump Cuba Sanctions
summary: A Canadian miner that is one of Cuba’s biggest foreign investors warned investors that its ability to carry on as a going concern is in doubt after Donald Trump expanded US sanctions on the Caribbean nation.

title: 'Chinamaxxing' bets at Mobile World Congress Shanghai
summary: A lot of mobile and consumer brands at the Mobile World Congress in Shanghai are taking advantage of the "Chinamaxxing" trend of targeting younger consumers abroad. Bloomberg's Chief North Asia correspondent Stephen Engle has more. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Half of Australian Adults Have Never Sought Retirement Advice
summary: Australians are sleepwalking into their retirement, according to new research.

title: Veja os Três Assuntos Que Marcaram a Semana no Brasil
summary:
A repórter Patricia Xavier conta os assuntos que marcaram o país nesta semana. Com edição de Bárbara Nascimento e produção de Meg Lopes, Catarina Bacci e Mariana Lumy.

 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Chinese Energy Firm Pushes Fresh Debt Revamp After Payment Delay
summary: Huachen Energy Co. plans to present a proposal to restructure its bonds, including $627 million in dollar-denominated notes, to investors as early as August, according to people familiar with the matter, as the Chinese power producer makes a fresh bid to ease liquidity pressure.

title: Cathay Bank Chairman Quits After Governance Dispute, Family Feud
summary: Cathay United Bank Co. Chairman Kuo Ming-jian has resigned following a regulatory breach at the lender’s asset management affiliate linked to his outside positions, an incident that culminated in a physical altercation with a member of Taiwan’s billionaire Tsai family.

title: Trump Touts $11.1 Billion Aid Request to Woo Struggling Farmers
summary: President Donald Trump had a surprise for the farmers he invited to dinner at the White House on Thursday: a request to Congress for $11.1 billion in assistance that would amount to the second bailout of the year for the beleaguered agriculture sector.

title: Oil Prices Rise as Ship Struck in Hormuz
summary: Oil prices turned higher after a ship was hit by an unknown projectile in the Strait of Hormuz, sustaining damage to the bridge. Bloomberg's Mica Soellner has the context. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: BHP’s Incoming CEO Craig Tweaks Leadership Team Before Start
summary: BHP Group’s incoming Chief Executive Officer Brandon Craig has announced changes to the company’s leadership team before he officially assumes the role on July 1.

title: China’s Hardware Tech Stocks Look to Earnings to Sustain Rally
summary: Chinese hardware technology stocks have been on a tear. The next challenge is showing the earnings to back it up.

title: Mideast Oil Boost Gathers Pace as Qatar Sells Crude to Asia
summary: Qatar has joined other Persian Gulf nations in reviving crude oil sales, with regional producers cranking up activity as peace talks between the US and Iran progress.
